summary: There are always five philosophers and five callbacks per request, with at most three hundred requests, so safety and progress checks replace an inapplicable per-call scaling regression.

workload_bound.source_constraint: five philosophers each request food n times for 1 through 60

replacement_checks.evidence: Every invocation performs exactly two pick, one eat, and two put callbacks.
Authored cases cover reversed, adjacent-first, repeated-philosopher, randomized, and maximum-round starts.
Instrumentation verifies fork ownership, callback order, exactly-once events, and completed request counts.
All worker threads execute inside an isolated subprocess with bounded joins and a hard wall timeout.
